daily_brief: Friday's developments center on two critical diplomatic flashpoints with global implications. In the Gulf, US and Iranian negotiators have agreed on a framework to extend their fragile ceasefire by 60 days, though President Trump has not yet signed off on the deal [BBC, Financial Times, The Hindu]. The proposed agreement would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift oil sanctions on Iran, with markets in Tokyo and Seoul surging to record highs on the news [Nikkei Asia]. Meanwhile, NATO territory was directly impacted when a Russian drone struck an apartment building in Romania, injuring two people—the first time citizens of the alliance have been hurt by such incursions since the Ukraine war began [BBC, Al Jazeera, France 24, Financial Times]. NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte condemned Russia's 'reckless behaviour,' while Romania called for accelerated anti-drone capabilities. In Gaza, Prime Minister Netanyahu announced plans to expand Israeli territorial control to 70%, contradicting the October 2025 ceasefire terms [BBC]. A Kenyan court suspended US plans to establish an Ebola quarantine facility on its soil [RFI, The Guardian], as the WHO chief arrived in the DRC to coordinate response to an outbreak that has killed over 220 people. In Austria, a man was sentenced to 15 years for plotting an attack on Taylor Swift's Vienna concert [BBC].

top_stories.headline: US and Iran Near 60-Day Ceasefire Extension, Awaiting Trump Approval
bullets: US and Iranian negotiators have agreed on a memorandum of understanding to extend the ceasefire for 60 days and begin talks on Iran's nuclear program, though President Trump has not yet approved the deal

The proposed framework would allow unrestricted passage through the Strait of Hormuz, give Iran 30 days to remove mines from the waterway, lift the US blockade, and issue sanction waivers for Iranian oil sales
Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency said the deal has not been finalized or confirmed by Tehran
Vice President Vance stated the sides are 'very close' but 'not there yet,' with several sticking points remaining

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Detailed focus on diplomatic process and both sides' positions

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline framing emphasizes US progress toward deal

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provides live updates emphasizing volatility of talks and mutual accusations of violations

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Focuses on market reaction, with Japanese and Korean stocks hitting record highs

top_stories.headline: Russian Drone Strikes Romanian Apartment Building, Injuring Two in First NATO Casualty Incident
bullets: A Russian drone crashed into a 10th-floor apartment in Galati, eastern Romania, near the Ukrainian border, injuring two people and triggering a fire that required evacuation of approximately 70 residents
This marks the first time citizens of a NATO member state have been injured by Russian drone incursions during the Ukraine war
NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte condemned 'Russia's reckless behaviour' and said the alliance 'stands ready to defend every inch of Allied territory'
Romania requested NATO accelerate transfer of anti-drone capabilities and convened an emergency Supreme Defence Council meeting

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes NATO and EU official responses

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Notes pattern of incursions across Baltic and Nordic NATO states

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Includes Ukrainian foreign ministry statement on broader regional threat

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline treatment without accessible full article

top_stories.headline: Netanyahu Orders IDF to Expand Gaza Control to 70%, Contradicting Ceasefire Terms
bullets: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced he has directed the IDF to increase territorial control of Gaza from 60% to 70%
The expansion contradicts the October 2025 Trump-brokered ceasefire, under which Israel agreed to withdraw to a demarcation line at 53% control
At least 738 Palestinians have been killed since the ceasefire took effect, according to the Hamas-run health ministry
Indirect US-brokered talks between Israel and Hamas on advancing Trump's peace plan remain deadlocked

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting of Netanyahu's statements and ceasefire context

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Companion piece focuses on humanitarian conditions, specifically healthcare access in Gaza

top_stories.headline: Blue Origin's New Glenn Rocket Explodes During Test at Cape Canaveral
bullets: Blue Origin's New Glenn heavy-lift rocket exploded during a hotfire test on the launch pad at Cape Canaveral, Florida, on Thursday evening
The company confirmed no injuries occurred and all personnel were accounted for
Jeff Bezos stated the company would 'rebuild whatever needs rebuilding' while acknowledging it was a 'very rough day'
The New Glenn rocket is central to Blue Origin's commercial spaceflight and lunar mission ambitions

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Contextualizes as setback for Bezos's space ambitions, notes Elon Musk's condolences

top_stories.headline: Kenyan Court Suspends US Plan for Ebola Quarantine Facility as Outbreak Spreads
bullets: A Kenyan high court ordered temporary suspension of US plans to open a 50-bed Ebola quarantine facility at Laikipia Air Base for American citizens exposed to the virus
The Katiba Institute challenged the plan, citing constitutional concerns over rights to life, health, and lack of parliamentary oversight
The WHO has recorded over 220 suspected Ebola deaths in the DRC since the outbreak was declared on May 15, with WHO Director-General Tedros arriving in the country to support containment efforts
Uganda has closed its border with DRC for four weeks; the US has banned recent travelers from DRC, Uganda, and South Sudan from entering the country

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focuses on Kenyan court ruling and constitutional concerns

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes Trump administration policy of not bringing exposed Americans home and impact of aid cuts on outbreak response

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Additional coverage of WHO chief's visit and ceasefire call

additional_stories.headline: Austrian man sentenced to 15 years for plotting jihadist attack on Taylor Swift's Vienna concert
France's National Assembly unanimously votes to formally repeal the Code Noir, the 1685 slavery law
Hungary's new PM Peter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels seeking release of €18 billion in frozen funds
South Korea's SK Hynix becomes third Asian company to reach $1 trillion market capitalization amid AI chip boom
France to investigate reports of abuse including sexual violence against nationals detained on Gaza flotilla
Man arrested after stabbing attack injures three at Swiss train station in Winterthur; authorities cite extremism concerns
Documentary 'Prime Minister' about former New Zealand PM Jacinda Ardern wins top Emmy for best documentary
Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls' school kills at least 16 students in Nakuru county
Former Hong Kong Journalists Association chairman begins five-day prison sentence for obstructing police officer
Canadian PM Mark Carney calls for new US partnership ahead of USMCA review, emphasizes Canadian 'strategic autonomy'
